Typical beam currents were 800 nA.
The neutrons were detected in 16 detectors placed
between 0 and 82.5 degr. at intervals of 5 and 7.5 degr
at a flight path of 17.5 m.

The target for the argon isotope measurements was a
tantalum-lined aluminium cylinder, 3 cm diameter*1 cm
high, with a 4.5 mg/cm2 tantalum entrance foil. This
cell was filled with enriched and cleaned 36,38,40Ar
gases at pressures of 88, 77 and 97 Torr, respectively,
which resulted in target thicknesses of 186, 129 and
225 mu-g/cm2, respectively.

Fig.6
The calcium targets were made by evaporating layers of
calcium onto 0.2 mm thick tungsten backings. The 40Ca
target (natural isotopic abundance) was 196 mu-g/cm2
thick, and the 42Ca target (enrichment to better than
99%) was 400 mu-g/cm2 thick.
Fig.7-9
The calcium targets were made by evaporating layers of
